sources.exact_document_title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
sources.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
sources.clinical_setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documentation fields and continuity of anesthesia records. It supports recording clinician-assessed facts and completed plans, not generating management, medicines, doses, procedures, referrals, discharge, or urgency.

exact_sections.heading: Documentation across the anesthesia care continuum
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports accurate, complete, and legible anesthesia documentation across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.

exact_sections.heading: Preanesthesia documentation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

exact_sections.heading: Intraoperative or procedural anesthesia documentation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports re-evaluation, equipment, medicines, staff, monitoring, techniques, positioning, lines and procedures, unusual events, fluids or blood products, output, and transfer status as recorded facts.

exact_sections.heading: Postanesthesia documentation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports PACU admission and discharge records, discharge criteria, complications, postanesthesia evaluation, and documented recovery status.

archive.title: Statement on Documentation of Anesthesia Care
archive.population: Patients receiving anesthesia services across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.
archive.setting: Anesthesia care continuum and anesthesia medical records.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Documentation across the anesthesia care continuum
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports accurate, complete, and legible anesthesia documentation across preanesthesia, intraoperative, and postanesthesia phases.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Preanesthesia documentation
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Intraoperative or procedural anesthesia documentation
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports re-evaluation, equipment, medicines, staff, monitoring, techniques, positioning, lines and procedures, unusual events, fluids or blood products, output, and transfer status as recorded facts.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Postanesthesia documentation
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports PACU admission and discharge records, discharge criteria, complications, postanesthesia evaluation, and documented recovery status.

sources.population: Patients receiving planned day surgery or procedure services in DoH-licensed facilities, with age, risk, procedure, anesthesia, and discharge qualifications.
sources.clinical_setting: DoH-licensed day surgery and procedure facilities in Abu Dhabi.
sources.applicability_note: UAE-direct for service governance, assessment, care plans, pre/intra/post-procedure documentation, records, discharge, and patient education. It does not replace anesthesiologist judgment, facility policy, consent, credentialing, or emergency care.

exact_sections.heading: Scope, definitions, and service requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the licensed day-surgery and procedure service, eligible service scope, and facility responsibilities.

exact_sections.heading: Governance, workforce, policies, and risk controls
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting governance, credentialing, safety, escalation, infection-control, medication, emergency, and quality policy context without generating a clinical action.

exact_sections.heading: Patient assessment and individualized care plan
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting pre-procedure assessment, history, examination, risk, suitability, investigations, consent, and an individualized clinician-entered care plan.

exact_sections.heading: Pre-procedure requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting identity, procedure, site, consent, fasting, preparation, medicines, allergy, and pre-procedure checks when assessed or completed.

exact_sections.heading: Intra-procedure requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports procedure, monitoring, equipment, staff, events, and clinical-record documentation during a procedure without directing treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recovery and discharge requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recovery observation, pain or nausea assessment, discharge criteria, instructions, escort, complications, handover, and follow-up only as clinician-completed documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Health-record requirements
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports complete, contemporaneous records for assessment, consent, anesthesia or sedation, procedure, monitoring, recovery, discharge, complications, and follow-up.

exact_sections.heading: Patient education and communication
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports patient-centred explanation, understandable language, interpreter support, written information, questions, and documented education.
